perturbation.original_text: The Parties may withdraw from this JV only for serious and justified reasons or by mutual consent. The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties may withdraw from this JV only for any reasons or by mutual consent. The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the reasons for withdrawal from the JV. The original text restricts withdrawal to 'serious and justified reasons,' providing a clear, though subjective, criterion. The modified version allows withdrawal for 'any reasons,' which completely removes the restriction. This contradicts the spirit of the original clause, creating uncertainty in enforcement, as it is unclear what constitutes a valid withdrawal and opens the door for arbitrary termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WITHDRAWAL AND RESOLUTION The Parties may withdraw from this JV only for any reasons or by mutual consent. The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days. Withdrawal or termination by mutual consent shall only have effect for the future and shall not affect the part of the Agreement already executed. In case of withdrawal according to the previous paragraph, Kiromic is obliged to cover Molipharma for the expenses incurred and for those committed, related to the research programs being developed, until receipt of the notice of withdrawal. -8- Pursuant to art. 1456 of the Italian Civil Code, this JV shall be terminated by right in the following cases: a. Breach of confidentiality obligations; b. Unilateral and unagreed variation of the Scope of the JV; c. Failure of each Party to comply with its obligations, including the economic commitments. The Party concerned must communicate by registered letter with return receipt, or PEC, its intention to avail itself of the termination clause. In the event of termination of the Agreement pursuant to this clause or, in any case, to termination due to Kiromic's default, the same is required; in addition, Kiromic undertakes to the reimbursement of expenses incurred and/or committed by Molipharma, and agrees to recognize financially the additional damage suffered by Molipharma by such a default. Upon termination of the contract, the agreement set forth in clause 5 ("Intellectual property rights and prohibition of transfer to third parties") and clause 6 ("Economic rights") will remain into force.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the condition for withdrawal from 'serious and justified reasons' to 'any reasons.' This directly contradicts the original intent and creates a clear inconsistency, as it eliminates a previously defined restriction. The contradicted text, 'The Parties may withdraw from this JV only for any reasons or by mutual consent,' which is the same as the Changed Text, is also contradicted because the overall intention of the contract was for withdrawal only for 'serious and justified reasons,' as mentioned in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: All economics rights are solely owned by Kiromic Biopharma. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the follows royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe.
perturbation.changed_text: All economic rights are jointly owned by Kiromic Biopharma and Molipharma with equal shares. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the following royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 7. Initially, it states that all economic rights for Oncology are solely owned by Kiromic. However, the very next sentence indicates that Kiromic will grant royalties to Molipharma from the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results. By changing the ownership clause to joint ownership and maintain the same next sentence about royalties to Molipharma, now this clause contradicts with the rest of the section. The original text already had implied an inconsistency, but the modification to joint ownership escalates this conflict. The contradiction makes the enforcement of economic rights unclear, as it's uncertain how joint ownership aligns with royalty payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ECONOMIC RIGHTS The commercial rights arising from the research programs referred to in point 2 are divided as follows: Oncology All economic rights are jointly owned by Kiromic Biopharma and Molipharma with equal shares. Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the following royalties: - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Italy; -7- - *% of the realized turnover by the marketing of Ovarian Cancer research results in Europe. Sars-cov-2 - The economic rights for Europa will be an exclusive ownership of Molipharma - The economic rights in the U.S. will be an exclusive ownership of Kiromic. - For the rest of the world, the economic rights will be divided as follows: *% Kiromic; *% Molipharma.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'All economic rights are jointly owned by Kiromic Biopharma and Molipharma with equal shares,' but then continues to say 'Kiromic will grant to Molipharma the following royalties.' This creates a contradiction because if economic rights are jointly owned with equal shares, it doesn't make sense for one party (Kiromic) to 'grant' royalties to the other (Molipharma). Royalties imply that one party owns the rights and is compensating the other for their use, which is inconsistent with joint ownership. This is directly conflicting within the stated sections, and could cause conflict of understanding to ownership.

perturbation.original_text: If one of the Parties has no interest in applying for a patent, it will inform the other Party within 30 days from the communication of the Results referred to in paragraph 1. In this case the Party concerned shall have the right to proceed with the submission of the application on the Results at its own expense and in co-ownership with the other Party, subject to written notice. The Party which is not interested in the application shall undertake to transfer its own share of ownership to the other Party, free of charge once it has obtained the patent title.
perturbation.changed_text: If one of the Parties has no interest in applying for a patent, it will inform the other Party within 30 days from the communication of the Results referred to in paragraph 1. In this case, both parties shall equally share the right to proceed with the submission of the application on the Results at each party's own expense and in co-ownership with the other Party, subject to written notice. The Party which is not interested in the application shall undertake to transfer its own share of ownership to the other Party, free of charge once it has obtained the patent title.
perturbation.explanation: The change in this section creates a contradiction by altering the procedure for patent application when one party is not interested. Originally, the party *concerned* had the *sole* right to proceed. The modified text states that *both* parties *equally share* the right to proceed, at *each party's own expense*, even if only one initially expressed interest. This contradicts the purpose of the original clause, creating uncertainty: it's unclear if the party that expressed no interest still has to share expenses and co-manage the patent application.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND PROHIBITION OF TRANSFER TO THIRD PARTIES The Parties undertake to promptly notify each other about the achievement of the Scope, as mentioned in point 2 ("the Results") , that may be subject to Industrial and Intellectual Property Rights, within 30 days from the achievement of such Results and to cooperate in the evaluation of the existence of the necessary requirements for the patenting/registration of such Results. The Industrial Property Rights on the Results, as well as the Intellectual Property Rights realized in the research activities covered by this JV, are due jointly to the parties in equal shares (50% for each Party), without prejudice to the possibility of agreeing in writing, during the course of every specific activity, about the modification of the respective shares of co-ownership, based upon the actual contribution of each of the Parties to the research activities, and also without prejudice to the recognition of the intellectual rights due to each inventor pursuant to current legislation. The parties will agree, by separate agreement, on the specific discipline relating to the management of rights in co-ownership; it is agreed that Molipharma may always use the Results for teaching and research purposes. If one of the Parties has no interest in applying for a patent, it will inform the other Party within 30 days from the communication of the Results referred to in paragraph 1. In this case, both parties shall equally share the right to proceed with the submission of the application on the Results at each party's own expense and in co-ownership with the other Party, subject to written notice. The Party which is not interested in the application shall undertake to transfer its own share of ownership to the other Party, free of charge once it has obtained the patent title. -6- Each Party is the owner of the Industrial and Intellectual Property Rights relating to its own: a. "Background": All knowledge, information and intangible assets protected under national Law System and international intellectual and industrial property laws and regulations, created or otherwise obtained by a Party prior to the begin of the activity covered by this Agreement. b. "Sideground": All knowledge, information and intangible property protected under national Law System and international intellectual and industrial property laws and regulations made or otherwise obtained by a Party during the term of this Agreement but not in the execution of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Parties shall grant each other, free of charge, a non-exclusive right to use their respective Backgrounds in connection with the activities which will be carried out by this JV and by reason of their execution. This right is granted for the duration of the Agreement only, with the express denial of sublicensing or transferring it to any third party for any reason whatsoever. The Sideground of each Party may not be used by the other Party without the express written authorization of the owner. The sale, licensing or any other type of agreement providing for the transfer, even temporary, to third parties of intellectual and industrial property rights deriving from the research programs referred to in point 2 is excluded, unless there is prior agreement between the Parties. Kiromic assigns to Molipharma all the rights of publication of the research, unless they are considered confidential for patenting. To this purpose, before each publication, Molipharma will send in advance the text of the publication to Kiromic for approval. The consent of Kiromic will be tacitly granted after 30 days from receipt of the request for authorization of disclosure. The same procedure indicated in the previous paragraph will be also applied to Kiromic in case it wants to perform a publication on the research.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that if one party is not interested in a patent, the interested party can proceed alone. The changed text mandates that both parties *equally* share the right to proceed, *at each party's own expense*, regardless of one party's initial disinterest. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates a contradiction within the 'INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS' section as it forces an unwanted obligation on the party initially uninterested in the patent application, disrupting the initial allocation of rights.
